info.title: Educly Edge Functions API
info.summary: Swagger/OpenAPI documentation for Educly serverless APIs.
info.description:
This project exposes its API surface primarily through Supabase Edge Functions under `/functions/v1/*`.

Documented from the current source code on March 19, 2026. Source of truth: `supabase/functions/*/index.ts`.

Important: some routes are clearly intended for internal service-to-service usage but currently do not enforce transport-level auth inside the function handler. These cases are flagged in each operation description so the docs stay honest to the implementation.

tags.name: Public Auth
tags.description: Public-facing signup, password recovery and access-link routes.

tags.name: User Chat
tags.description: Authenticated chat and assistant endpoints used by students.

tags.name: User Features
tags.description: Authenticated user endpoints outside chat, such as certificates and targeted lifecycle emails.

tags.name: Admin
tags.description: Admin-only operational endpoints guarded by an authenticated admin JWT.

tags.name: Email / Messaging
tags.description: Transactional or batch email orchestration endpoints.

tags.name: Webhooks
tags.description: Incoming payment and CRM webhook receivers.

tags.name: Operations
tags.description: Cron, retry, diagnostics and maintenance endpoints.

adminBearer.description: Authenticated Supabase user JWT for an admin account.

paddleSignature.name: paddle-signature
paddleSignature.description: Alternative Paddle verification header. Must validate against `PADDLE_WEBHOOK_SECRET`.

cronBearer.description: Cron or internal bearer token. Used by `retry-failed-webhooks` with `CRON_SECRET` or service role.

Forbidden.description: Authenticated, but not allowed to use this route.

MethodNotAllowed.description: HTTP method not supported by this endpoint.

language.description: IETF language tag or short language code.

locale.description: Browser or app locale, for example `pt-BR` or `en`.

language.description: Optional. Present in the handler interface, but the function currently derives the actual email locale from profile/user metadata.

secret.description: Alternative to bearer auth. Must match `CRON_SECRET`.

post.summary: Grant product access to a user manually
post.description:
Admin-only route used to manually grant one or more product entitlements and premium access to a user.

Source: `supabase/functions/admin-grant-access/index.ts`.

post.summary: Revoke selected products from a user
post.description:
Admin-only route that revokes active product access rows and recalculates premium status.

Source: `supabase/functions/admin-revoke-access/index.ts`.

post.summary: AI Hub chat with multiple assistant personas
post.description:
Authenticated student chat endpoint for the AI Hub. Streams text responses for most personas and returns JSON when `aiType=nanobanana` triggers image generation.

Source: `supabase/functions/assistentes-chat/index.ts`.

messages.content: Explique o que é prompt engineering.

200.description: SSE stream for text personas, or JSON creative payload for `nanobanana`.

schema.description: Server-Sent Events stream compatible with OpenAI-style chat completion deltas.

example.text: Vou criar uma imagem futurista inspirada no seu pedido.

post.summary: Create or reconcile an account and return permanent access token
post.description:
Creates a new user or reconciles an existing one, processes pending billing events and returns a permanent access token from `user_access_tokens`.

Warning: the current handler does not enforce auth inside the function and should be treated as internal service-to-service only.

Source: `supabase/functions/auto-create-account/index.ts`.

post.summary: Bulk import billing grants from CSV-style product strings
post.description:
Admin-only route that queues or immediately processes grant events for up to 50 entries per call.

Source: `supabase/functions/bulk-grant-access/index.ts`.

post.summary: Expire premium and product access via database RPC
post.description:
Operational route that calls `check_and_expire_access` and returns counts/details.

Intended for cron/manual maintenance. Current code allows invocation without strict auth; recommended usage is internal only, optionally with `x-cron-job: true`.

Source: `supabase/functions/check-access-expiration/index.ts`.

parameters.name: x-cron-job
parameters.description: Optional hint used by the handler to identify cron execution.

post.summary: Auto-confirm a recently created purchased account
post.description:
Confirms a user's email when the account was created recently and a purchase exists for that email.

Warning: the current handler does not enforce transport auth and should only be used internally.

Source: `supabase/functions/confirm-signup-email/index.ts`.

get.summary: Inspect billing state for a single email
get.description:
Diagnostic endpoint used to inspect recent billing events, product access and premium status for an email.

Source: `supabase/functions/debug-billing/index.ts`.

post.summary: Generate and store a course certificate PNG
post.description:
Authenticated student endpoint that renders a PNG certificate, uploads it to Supabase Storage and stores a certificate row in the database.

Source: `supabase/functions/generate-certificate/index.ts`.

example.contribuicao: em projetos reais de IA generativa

post.summary: Premium student EDI/academy chat
post.description:
Authenticated premium chat endpoint used inside the academy experience. Validates premium access and streams model output.

Source: `supabase/functions/iacademy-chat/index.ts`.

messages.content: Quais ferramentas de IA eu devo aprender primeiro?

schema.description: Server-Sent Events stream compatible with OpenAI-style chat completion deltas.

post.summary: Public landing-page support chat
post.description:
Public chat endpoint used on the marketing/landing surface. May answer with keyword-based quick replies or stream model output.

Source: `supabase/functions/landing-support-chat/index.ts`.

messages.content: Quais ferramentas de IA a Educly ensina?

200.description: SSE stream or synthetic SSE fallback response.

schema.description: Server-Sent Events stream compatible with OpenAI-style chat completion deltas.

get.summary: Resolve permanent token into a fresh Supabase magic link
get.description:
Accepts a permanent access token via query string and returns a fresh `action_link` generated through Supabase Auth.

Source: `supabase/functions/magic-login/index.ts`.

post.summary: Resolve permanent token into a fresh Supabase magic link
post.description:
Same contract as the GET variant, but reads the token from JSON body.

Source: `supabase/functions/magic-login/index.ts`.

post.summary: Create an account for a pending user
post.description:
Creates a user without requiring purchase existence, but returns conflict if the user already exists. Used for pending-access onboarding flows.

Source: `supabase/functions/pending-signup/index.ts`.

post.summary: Create an account only when a purchase already exists
post.description:
Creates a user only if `check_purchase_exists` succeeds for the email. Returns `403 NO_PURCHASE` when no qualifying purchase exists.

Source: `supabase/functions/purchased-signup/index.ts`.

403.description: No purchase found for this email.

example.error: No purchase found for this email

post.summary: Send a new permanent-access magic link email
post.description:
Looks up the user by email, reuses or creates a permanent access token and sends a new email through Resend.

Source: `supabase/functions/resend-magic-link/index.ts`.

post.summary: Send password reset email
post.description:
Generates a Supabase recovery link and sends a localized email via Resend. The handler intentionally returns `200 { success: true }` even when the user is missing or an internal error occurs, to avoid account enumeration.

Source: `supabase/functions/send-password-reset/index.ts`.

post.summary: Trigger day-5 upsell email for the authenticated user
post.description:
Authenticated user route that decides whether to email the current student about AI Hub or Freelancer upsell after day 5.

Source: `supabase/functions/send-upsell-day5/index.ts`.

200.description: Email sent or skipped with reason.

post.summary: Receive Paddle billing and customer webhooks
post.description:
Processes Paddle events, resolves customer email, logs billing actions, reconciles entitlements and triggers post-purchase account/email flows.

Authentication is accepted either through `Authorization: Bearer <PADDLE_API_KEY>` or `paddle-signature` verified against `PADDLE_WEBHOOK_SECRET`.

Source: `supabase/functions/paddle-webhook/index.ts`.

schema.description: Raw Paddle webhook payload. Event shape varies by `event_type` and is passed through mostly intact.

200.description: Webhook accepted, ignored, deduplicated or processed successfully.

post.summary: Receive Hotmart / Primer / FunnelFox purchase webhooks
post.description:
Receives purchase events, logs billing actions, reconciles entitlements, and orchestrates welcome or magic-link delivery.

Authentication is accepted either through `x-hotmart-hottok` or `ff-webhook-signature`.

Source: `supabase/functions/primer-webhook/index.ts`.

schema.description: Raw Hotmart/FunnelFox payload. Event shape varies by provider.

post.summary: Resend onboarding emails to recent USER_NOT_FOUND billing records
post.description:
Internal batch route protected by a shared secret in the request body. Rebuilds welcome emails for recent `USER_NOT_FOUND` events.

Source: `supabase/functions/resend-pending-emails/index.ts`.

post.summary: Retry failed webhook deliveries
post.description:
Cron/internal route that replays failed webhook payloads against the original target edge function. Auth is accepted either as `Authorization: Bearer <CRON_SECRET|service_role>` or as body `{ secret }`.

Source: `supabase/functions/retry-failed-webhooks/index.ts`.

post.summary: Send a bulk welcome-style email blast to users
post.description:
Internal route protected by a body secret. Iterates over users (optionally filtered by `targetEmails`) and sends a transactional HTML email through Resend.

Source: `supabase/functions/send-bulk-emails/index.ts`.

post.summary: Send a one-off incident resolution notification to premium users
post.description:
Operational route used to notify a fixed window of premium users about a resolved incident.

Warning: current implementation does not enforce auth inside the function and should be treated as internal-only.

Source: `supabase/functions/send-incident-notification/index.ts`.

post.summary: Process queued pending thank-you / welcome emails
post.description:
Cron/internal batch route that groups `pending_thank_you_emails` by email, creates/reconciles accounts when possible, and dispatches welcome or magic-link emails.

Warning: current implementation does not enforce auth inside the function and should be treated as internal-only.

Source: `supabase/functions/send-pending-thanks/index.ts`.

post.summary: Admin-triggered batch for pending welcome emails
post.description:
Admin-only route that scans pending billing events, creates/reconciles accounts and sends welcome or magic-link emails in controlled batches.

Source: `supabase/functions/send-pending-welcome-batch/index.ts`.

post.summary: Admin-triggered signup invite emails
post.description:
Admin-only route that sends signup invitation emails to a list of addresses and tracks opens through `track-email-open`.

Source: `supabase/functions/send-signup-invite/index.ts`.

post.summary: Send welcome or magic-link email
post.description:
Transactional email route used by purchase/signup workflows. Supports legacy CTA email, new-account magic-link email, and existing-account magic-link email.

Warning: the current handler does not enforce auth inside the function and should be treated as internal service-to-service only.

Source: `supabase/functions/send-welcome-email/index.ts`.

get.description:
Returns a transparent GIF and updates `email_logs.opened_at` when `id` is present.

Source: `supabase/functions/track-email-open/index.ts`.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Email log identifier. If omitted or invalid, the route still returns the pixel.
